quality.rationale: 结合视界极限分析与四动量投影关系，逻辑链条完整且具备真实的黑洞自旋与拖曳物理背景

origin.question: Problem 17.10. Show that as a test particle approaches $\mathbf{r} = \mathbf{r}_{+}$ , the horizon of a Kerr black hole, it has "angular velocity as seen from infinity" equal to$$$\Omega \equiv \frac {\mathrm {d} \phi}{\mathrm {d} t} = \frac {\mathrm {a}}{2 \mathrm {M r}_{+}}$.
origin.solution: Solution 17.10. In Problem 17.8 we saw that $\Omega \to \mathfrak{a} / 2\mathbf{M}\mathbf{r}_{+}$ when $\theta = \pi /2$ To prove this is so for any $\theta$ we write, in the notation of Problem 17.9:$$$\Omega = \frac {\mathrm {p}^{\phi}}{\mathrm {p}^{\mathrm {t}}} = \frac {\mathrm {g}^{\phi \phi} \mathrm {p}_{\phi} + \mathrm {g}^{\phi \mathrm {t}} \mathrm {p}_{\mathrm {t}}}{\mathrm {g}^{\mathrm {t t}} \mathrm {p}_{\mathrm {t}} + \mathrm {g}^{\phi \mathrm {t}} \mathrm {p}_{\phi}} = \omega - \mathrm {e}^{2 \nu} \frac {\mathrm {e}^{- 2 \psi} \mathrm {p}_{\phi}} {\mathrm {p}_{\mathrm {t}} + \omega \mathrm {p}_{\phi}}$.At $\Delta = 0$ (that is, on the horizon) the metric function $e^{-2\psi}$ is finite but $e^{2\nu}$ vanishes, hence $\Omega = \omega$ and at $\Delta = 0$$$$\omega = \frac {- g_{\phi t}} {g_{\phi \phi}} = \frac {a} {2 M r_{+}}$.
